content.full_text:
City of Berk eley
Fair Campaign Practices Commission
Martin Luther King, Jr.
Civic Center Building
2180 Milvia Street
Berkeley, California 94704
(41s) 644-6380
TTY 644-691s
August 21, 1991
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 I'Jtr Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 9581 4
Re: Request for Advice Letter
Dear Colleague:
I am writing to you on behalf of the City of Berkeley's Fair
Campaign Practices Commission (FCPC). During the November 1990
election and following Judge Karltonrs ruling in SEIU v. FPPC,
the FCPC received several inquiries from candidat.es regarding the
applicability of Government Code section 85304 to candidat.es for
local municipal elections. At that time, I was verbally advised
that because the City of Berkeley had a local ordinance which
limited campaign contributions to $250 per election, Government
Code section 85304 prohibited local candidates from having multi-
candidate controlled committees and from transferring campaign
contributions to other candidates. The FCPC would like to
clarify for locaI candidates whether this interpretation of
Section 85304 is still 
correct and therefore requests that your
response be in the form of a writter^ a,ivice ietter.
If you need further information, please feel free to contact me.
Thank you for your cooperation.
Sincerely,
S"^tt^W
SARAH REYNOSO
FCPC Staff Counsel
SR: th
cc: 
FCPC Commissioners

sections.question: Whether Government Code section 85304 prohibits local candidates in Berkeley from having multi-candidate controlled committees and from transferring campaign contributions to other candidates, given that the City of Berkeley has a local ordinance limiting campaign contributions to $250 per election.
sections.question_synthetic: The Berkeley Fair Campaign Practices Commission seeks clarification on whether Government Code section 85304, in light of Judge Karlton's ruling in SEIU v. FPPC, prohibits local candidates from establishing multi-candidate controlled committees and transferring contributions when the local jurisdiction has a $250 contribution limit.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Berkeley FCPC to the state FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal Question or Conclusion sections present. Document requests written advice from FPPC on interpretation of section 85304 post-SEIU v. FPPC ruling.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether Government Code section 85304 prohibits local candidates in Berkeley from having multi-candidate controlled committees and from transferring campaign contributions to other candidates, given that the City of Berkeley has a local ordinance limiting campaign contributions to $250 per election.

During the November 1990 election and following Judge Karltonrs ruling in SEIU v. FPPC, the FCPC received several inquiries from candidat.es regarding the applicability of Government Code section 85304 to candidat.es for local municipal elections. At that time, I was verbally advised that because the City of Berkeley had a local ordinance which limited campaign contributions to $250 per election, Government Code section 85304 prohibited local candidates from having multi- candidate controlled committees and from transferring campaign contributions to other candidates. embedding.summary: The City of Berkeley's Fair Campaign Practices Commission requests an FPPC advice letter regarding the interpretation of Government Code section 85304 and its application to local candidates following a judicial ruling, specifically concerning whether candidates can have multi-candidate committees and transfer contributions under Berkeley's $250 contribution limit.
